title: phebalium carruthersii
intro: Phebalium carruthersii, also known as Carruthers' peppermint bush, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to Australia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Phebalium carruthersii include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
